goal: Add category capture pages for open source launch

summary.title: Add launch category-capture pages
summary.what: Added English category-capture assets for the open-source launch: a Mainline category page, coding agent memory and repo memory definition pages, the Mainline Agent Memory Eval page, a glossary, a design partner page, integration docs, additional comparison pages, and expanded sitemap and LLM discovery files.
summary.why: The user wanted the website to claim category ownership around coding agent memory, repo memory for AI agents, Git-native memory for coding agents, and engineering intent memory before formally open-sourcing Mainline.
summary.user_goal: Do the practical category-capture work that can ship now so the public launch has definition pages, benchmark positioning, integration keywords, comparison pages, and LLM-readable context.

decisions.chose: Add a generic CapturePage renderer backed by src/capturePages.ts and a static catch-all route.
decisions.rationale: The new launch pages share a consistent SEO/content structure, and a data-backed route lets the site add narrowly scoped definition, glossary, integration, and comparison pages without duplicating Astro markup.

decisions.chose: Make the new category-capture pages English-only for now with availableLocales set to en.
decisions.rationale: The site has zh and es support, but these launch pages were written under time pressure; suppressing unavailable language alternates avoids broken localized links and preserves room for later native copy review.

decisions.chose: Publish /eval/ with the 8-fixture, 3-seed, 9-to-0 live signal while explicitly calling it small, synthetic, directional, and not a universal model benchmark.
decisions.rationale: The user identified the eval as the highest-leverage category asset, but inherited context required avoiding overclaiming.

decisions.chose: Add integration docs for Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Windsurf, AGENTS.md workflows, and MCP workflows.
decisions.rationale: These pages target concrete agent-tool search intent while staying honest that Mainline is a CLI protocol and Git-native memory layer, not a vendor-specific extension.

decisions.chose: Expand llms.txt and add llms-full.txt with canonical pages, category terms, promptable phrases, boundaries, eval details, and integration URLs.
decisions.rationale: LLM search and answer engines need compact, explicit text that states what Mainline is, what category it defines, and what it is not.

decisions.chose: Keep AGENTS.md as opt-in policy and describe Mainline init as repo config, Git refs, skill, and hook setup rather than rewriting AGENTS.md.
decisions.rationale: The product boundary is that AGENTS.md can tell agents to call Mainline, while Mainline stores repo memory in its own Git-native records.

rejected.reason: The user explicitly warned against programmatic SEO; each integration page includes setup, agent-loop guidance, boundaries, or troubleshooting.
The existing multilingual risk calls for native-speaker review before high-traffic launch; English-only pages are safer than shipping stiff or inaccurate translations.
The source eval only supports a narrower directional claim about history-dependent failures such as abandoned approaches and superseded decisions.

acknowledged_constraints.note: Kept a hand-authored public /sitemap.xml for the Search Console target and added the new canonical category-capture URLs.
Did not add new zh/es category translations; new launch pages are English-only until native copy review is available.
New pages repeatedly explain Mainline as pre-edit repo memory and avoid copy that implies hard enforcement.

review_notes: The branch was rebased over origin/main before push; src/pages/es/blog/why-coding-agents-need-repo-memory.md and src/seoPages.ts appear in the intent-base diff from the remote typo PR, not from this category-capture implementation.
The public /sitemap.xml remains the Search Console target and now includes the new English category, eval, glossary, integration, and comparison pages.
Production deploy succeeded and https://mainline.sh returned 200 for /eval/, /design-partners/, /glossary/abandoned-approach/, /compare/code-search/, /llms-full.txt, and /sitemap.xml.

architectural_claims: Category-capture pages are generated as static English Astro pages from src/capturePages.ts through src/pages/[...capture].astro.
BaseLayout can restrict hreflang/language-switcher output per page through an availableLocales prop.
mainline.sh exposes both a compact /llms.txt and a fuller /llms-full.txt for LLM-readable product context.
behavioral_changes: New public routes exist for /mainline/, /use-cases/coding-agent-memory/, /use-cases/repo-memory-for-ai-agents/, /eval/, /design-partners/, glossary term pages, seven integration docs pages, and three additional comparison pages.
Footer links now surface Agent Memory Eval, Glossary, and Design partners, and the homepage design-partner CTA links to the design-partner page.
The Search Console sitemap target lists the new category-capture URLs.
llms.txt and llms-full.txt list the category terms, canonical pages, comparison summaries, integration docs, promptable phrases, and product boundaries.
security_implications: No forms, cookies, analytics, authentication, or user data collection were added.
New integration docs show existing CLI install and setup commands; they do not add a new installer endpoint.
migration_notes: New category-capture pages are English-only for now; localized variants should be added deliberately rather than linked before they exist.
The remote typo PR was rebased into the branch before this commit and is noted as inherited rebase context.
